formal_definition: The category of cryptoasset brought within the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026 (SI 2026/102)'s new regulated activities (operating a trading platform, dealing, safeguarding, arranging deals, staking, and -- for the 'qualifying stablecoin' subset -- issuance). The Regulations define the boundary of the term, and the FCA's own guidance distinguishes it from a 'relevant specified investment cryptoasset' (an asset, such as a tokenised share or bond, already regulated as a specified investment under other parts of the FSMA framework). The precise statutory definition text was not independently re-confirmed against the SI's own schedule during this research and should be checked directly before being quoted verbatim.
plain_definition: The UK's 2026 Regulations don't regulate every cryptocurrency or token -- only ones that meet the statutory definition of a 'qualifying cryptoasset'. It's the perimeter test that decides which activities (running a trading platform, dealing, safeguarding, arranging deals, staking, or issuing a qualifying stablecoin) actually need FCA authorisation once the regime is fully in force, on 25 October 2027.
